steps.title: Use the zero point to find k
steps.content:
Since the graph passes through $(-3, 0)$, we have $f(-3) = 0$. Substituting $x = -3$:
$$(-3 - 5)(-3 - 9)(-3 + k) = 0$$
The first two factors are $(-8)(-12) = 96 \neq 0$, so the third factor must equal zero:
$$-3 + k = 0$$
$$k = 3$$

steps.title: Evaluate f(0)
steps.content:
With $k = 3$, we have $f(x) = (x - 5)(x - 9)(x + 3)$. Evaluating at $x = 0$:
$$f(0) = (0 - 5)(0 - 9)(0 + 3)$$
$$f(0) = (-5)(-9)(3)$$
$$f(0) = 45 \cdot 3 = 135$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
